summary:
# Verification Report

**Verdict:** FAIL
**Claims reviewed:** 7
**Sources reviewed:** 0
**Open agenda items:** 1

## Findings
1. [HIGH] The Trump administration in 2025-2026 has taken a deregulatory approach to AI, aiming to foster innovation while addressing economic impacts. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
2. [HIGH] The Department of Government Efficiency has reduced approximately 75,000 federal jobs to increase efficiency with AI. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
3. [HIGH] Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell expressed caution over AI's impact on labor markets in March 2026. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
4. [MEDIUM] The Department of Labor has enhanced the Trade Adjustment Assistance program to aid workers displaced by AI. — Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
5. [HIGH] Klarna replaced 700 customer service roles with AI in 2024, impacting the professional services sector. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
6. [HIGH] Chegg's stock fell approximately 80% due to competition from AI tools like ChatGPT. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
7. [HIGH] Teleperformance shares fell 35% on AI disruption fears in 2023. — Core claim lacks two independent tier 1-3 sources. Claim has no linked sources or data artifacts.
